gate_question: Does this exact current protocol, with current live state, allow an unprivileged attacker to extract funds or rewards beyond entitlement?

candidate.title_or_claim: Missed-interval cap in _calculateIntervalsETHBurn allows attacker to claim 1.5% of entire ETH burn pool as incentive after 20+ days of inactivity
candidate.claimed_root_cause: When enough intervals are missed, amountPerInterval * (missedIntervals+1) exceeds totalETHBurn and is capped at totalETHBurn; the incentive in swapETHForTorusAndBurn is then 1.5% of the entire pool rather than 1.5% of a single interval allocation
candidate.claimed_impact: Attacker extracts ~0.033 ETH (1.5% of totalETHBurn ~2.22 ETH at current live state) in a single call after 20+ days of inactivity

live_preconditions.name: lastBurnedETHIntervalStartTimestamp
live_preconditions.observed_value: 1784021400 (recent; 20-day inactivity window not yet elapsed but is attacker-controlled by waiting)

source_code_basis.why_it_matters: Computes amountPerInterval = totalETHBurn * 500 / 10000 / 144 = totalETHBurn/2880; after 2880+ missed intervals (20+ days) amountPerInterval*(missedIntervals+1) > totalETHBurn and is capped at uint128(totalETHBurn), making the single-call allocation equal to the entire pool

Reads currInterval.amountAllocated (set to totalETHBurn by the cap), computes incentive = amountAllocated * 150 / 10000, then sends incentive ETH to msg.sender via low-level call; no secondary cap on incentive amount
INCENTIVE_FEE=150, BPS_DENOM=10000, INTERVALS_PER_DAY=144, INTERVAL_TIME=10 minutes; these confirm the 1.5% incentive rate and the 2880-interval threshold for cap trigger

call_sequence: 1. Wait until block.timestamp >= lastBurnedETHIntervalStartTimestamp + 20 days + 10 minutes (i.e., timeElapsed/INTERVAL_TIME - 1 >= 2880, so missedIntervals+1 >= 2881 > 2880)
4. _calculateIntervalsETHBurn returns _totalAmountForInterval = uint128(totalETHBurn) due to cap at L633-635
6. Back in swapETHForTorusAndBurn: amountAllocated = totalETHBurn; incentive = totalETHBurn * 150 / 10000
7. payable(msg.sender).call{value: incentive} sends 1.5% of entire ETH burn pool to attacker
state_after: totalETHBurn decreases by totalETHBurn (entire pool consumed in one call)

extraction_analysis.asset_or_reward: ETH from totalETHBurn pool held by TorusBuyAndProcess contract
extraction_analysis.attacker_gain: ~0.0333 ETH (1.5% of 2.22 ETH totalETHBurn at current live state)
extraction_analysis.victim_or_protocol_loss: Protocol loses 1.5% of entire ETH burn pool in a single call; normal per-interval entitlement would be 1.5% of totalETHBurn/2880 = ~0.0000115 ETH per call, making the amplification factor ~2880x
extraction_analysis.why_gain_exceeds_entitlement: The per-call incentive is designed to be 1.5% of one interval's allocation (~0.035% of pool per interval). The cap converts the allocation to 100% of the pool, making the incentive 1.5% of the entire pool. The IntervalAlreadyBurned guard only prevents replay within the same interval number; it does not prevent the cap from being reached. No secondary cap exists on the incentive amount itself.

setup: Target TorusBuyAndProcess at its deployed address (resolve from live_context)
local_proof_required.reject_if_assertion_fails: If the assertion fails (attacker does not receive ~1.5% of totalETHBurn), the cap-triggered full-pool incentive is not confirmed and the finding should be rejected. Also reject if the call reverts due to swap slippage or insufficient liquidity in the ETH->TitanX->TORUS path.
